findings.description: The PDF uses a two-column time/activity layout for the agenda schedule, while the HTML converts this into a table format. While the data is preserved, the visual structure changes significantly from indented time-activity pairs to a formal table with borders.
findings.evidence: PDF shows times (9:00 a.m., 9:10 a.m., etc.) left-aligned with activities to their right in a free-form layout. HTML renders this as a structured table with 'Time' and 'Activity' column headers and cell borders.

findings.description: In the Chairman's Report section, the sub-items (Letters, Ethics Training, etc.) are rendered as bulleted list items in HTML, whereas in the PDF they are indented sub-items without bullet markers.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Letters', 'Ethics Training and Statement of Economic Interest Reminder', etc. as indented text under Chairman's Report. HTML shows them with bullet points (•).

findings.description: The document uses h2 for 'AGENDA' which is acceptable, but h1 and h2 together represent the title. The heading hierarchy is reasonable given the document structure.
findings.evidence: h1: 'Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting', h2: 'AGENDA', h3: 'Thursday, May 14' — this is a defensible structural choice.

findings.description: The PDF does not present the agenda as a formal table with headers; it uses a time-based list format. The HTML converts this into a table with 'Time' and 'Activity' column headers, which are not in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF uses indented time-based list; HTML wraps it in a <table> with <th>Time</th> and <th>Activity</th> headers that don't exist in the source.

findings.description: The table column headers 'Time' and 'Activity' are added by the converter and do not appear in the source PDF. However, these are structural labels describing the content accurately rather than fabricated substantive content.
findings.evidence: HTML: <th>Time</th><th>Activity</th> — these words do not appear in the PDF but serve as descriptive structural labels.

findings.description: The page header 'Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda' and page footer with the asterisk note appear inline within a table cell (inside the 10:00 a.m. row), which is a minor structural artifact of the page break in the original PDF being embedded mid-content.
findings.evidence:
Inside the 10:00 a.m. table cell:
...page 1...
and
Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda
appear mid-cell.
